utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good morning ms. reyes !

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good morning .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how are you doing ma'am ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing well doctor , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i am fine thank you . so you've been having some problems with your right hip ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and where are you hurting ? can you show me ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right in the groin area .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and this has been going on since february 2020 ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and is it worse with movement ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well when it catches and i almost fall , yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so it kinda grabs you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , and this all started when you were walking ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , walking around the infusion room .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: so it started if i took a step back or , you know , stuff like that . now it happens anywhere .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , so now it hurts whenever you move ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it hurts when i pivot .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so if you pivot then it hurts , got it . um ...

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: anything can sometimes do it . sometimes it wo n't though , and sometimes it'll do it several times in a row .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: several times in a row , okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and sometimes i fall .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and you rate the pain to range from two through seven out of 10 ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , that's correct .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and are you experiencing fever or chills ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and any tingling or numbness ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and have you had any problems with your bowel or bladder ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and if you stay still , do you feel better ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , but i do n't want to stay still .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i understand , no problem . and for past medical history , do you have anything going on ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've had a lot of surgeries . i've had pcl , i had infertility , a gall bladder removed , but that's it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and for family history , it looks like there's high blood pressure , diabetes , thyroid disease , heart disease , kidney disease and gastric ulcers . for your current medications , it does n't look like you're taking anything at this time . and you're allergic to percocet , vicodin and regulin . and it looks like you've had intentional weight loss ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , i've lost 110 pounds .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that is awesome . and how did you do that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: with weight watchers .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that's great .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and how many months have you been participating in weight watchers ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i started in 2018 , and i've been at my current weight for a little over a year .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: that is awesome .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , very good , and congratulations . and so , for social history , it looks like you work at an infusion center ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , over at .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and you live with your roommate , no history of tobacco and you limit alcohol intake to less than five drinks per month .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's correct .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . well let's go ahead and take a look at your hip .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: please use my general physical exam template . physical exam . ms. reyes is a pleasant 56-year-old woman who is five feet , six inches in height , weighing 169 pounds . blood pressure is 115 over 75 . pulse rate is 67 . ankles , no ankle edema is noted , no calf tenderness . okay , ms. reyes , can you go ahead and stand up for me please and take a couple of steps ? great .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and can you walk on your tippy toes ? good , okay . and can you walk on your heels ? kind of a heel walk and toe walk are intact . um , go ahead and turn around please .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: examination of the cervical spine , any pain here now ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , no tenderness . look at your right and your left and then over to the right , then go ahead and look up , then look down , and look straight ahead . range of motion is full in the neck without pain . spurling's test is negative . exam of the low back . any pain here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . skin is intact , no midline tenderness to palpitation . go ahead and lean back . and lean to your right , to your left . does that hurt at all ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . and go ahead and bend forward and then come back up . and that does n't bother you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no . i did or do have several bulging discs .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . but you're not hurting right now ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , the weight loss has really decreased all the pain .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . range of motion is decreased in exertion . lateral flection without pain . any pain when i push ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . you can go ahead and , um , sit down please . no pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . sacroiliac signs are negative . examination of the hips . trochanteric is non tender . go ahead and lift your knee up , does that bother you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , just a little bit .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , little bit . and then back one , probably bothers you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right there , like there , yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . how about this way ? not too bad ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . range of motion is decreased in right hip with pain in the groin and internal and external rotation . okay , go ahead and keep it up , do n't let me push it down . does that hurt ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: right there .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . resisted right hip flection causes pain in the right groin region . no tenderness is noted . do you feel me touching you all the way down ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . motor control is normal in the lower extremities . go ahead and lift your knee up .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , lift it up . any pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and this one ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and squeeze your knees together , push it out and kick your leg out straight . now go ahead and bring it back and kick it out straight again . and go ahead and lean back , keep it loose . okay , all set . you can go ahead and sit up now .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you're welcome . so what i think we're dealing with is right hip degenerative joint disease .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and we do have some options . so first is to start some low impact exercises . i can provide you with a hand out with what exercises you can do . you should take nsaids as needed to help with the pain and discomfort , as well as use of a cane to help offload the right side . a cane will help support your painful side to help reduce the pain .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hmm , i do n't love that idea but i'll give it a try .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , that would be great . and we can also try a cortisone injection into the right hip joint to see if that offers any relief .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i would like to definitely get the injection .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . we can take care of that today while you're here and then schedule a follow up appointment in three months to see how you're doing , and then receive another injection if needed .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , that sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and here are the risks associated with getting the inje- injection . um , please just take a moment to review it and consent to the shot .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . we'll get that set up for ya . all right , well i hope things , um , feel better , and we will see you back here in three months .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: see you . have a nice day .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: thank you so much , you as well . deep tendon reflex is one plus throughout . no focal motor weakness is noted . no focal sensory deficit noted . can you please include the surgical list ? next radiographs , mr arthrogram of the right hip done june 3rd 2021 show high grade condromalacia involving the interosuperior right acetabulum with subchondral marrow edema and cyst formation . next paragraph plan . options include low impact exercise program , use of an nsaid and use of a cane to offload the right . we discussed that she'd like to proceed with the cortisone injection in right hip joint . i explained the risks of injection , including needles , sterile and covid . she understood and decided to proceed with the injection . she will follow up with me in three months for another injection if needed . end of dictation .

detail_list.summary: Right hip pain
The doctor asks the patient to identify the location of her pain.
The patient reports pain in the groin area.
The doctor asks if the pain started in February 2020.
The doctor asks if the pain is worse with movement.
The patient reports the hip catches, causing her to fall.
The doctor asks if the symptoms began while walking.
The patient reports symptoms began while walking in the infusion room.
The patient reports the pain now occurs with any movement.
The doctor asks if the patient has pain with any movement.
The patient reports pain when pivoting.
The patient reports falling on occasion due to hip pain.
The doctor asks if the pain intensity ranges from 2 to 7 out of 10.
The doctor asks if the patient has fever or chills.
The doctor asks if the patient has tingling or numbness.
The doctor asks if the patient has bowel or bladder dysfunction.
The doctor asks if the patient feels better when still.
The patient reports pain improves when still.
The doctor asks about the patient's past medical history.
The patient has a history of PCOS and cholecystectomy.
The patient is a 56-year-old female with a history of intentional weight loss.
The patient lost 110 pounds.
The patient has maintained her weight for over a year.
The patient works at an infusion center.
The patient lives with a roommate, denies tobacco use, and limits alcohol intake.
Physical exam findings include BP 115/75, pulse 67, and no ankle edema or calf tenderness.
Gait exam shows heel-walk and toe-walk are intact.
The doctor asks if the patient has cervical spine pain.
Cervical spine range of motion is full without pain and Spurling's test is negative.
Lumbar spine exam shows no midline tenderness.
The patient has a history of bulging discs.
The patient reports weight loss reduced her back pain.
Lumbar spine range of motion is decreased in extension and lateral flexion without pain.
Sacroiliac joints are nontender and trochanteric regions are nontender.
Right hip range of motion is decreased with pain in the groin on internal and external rotation.
Resisted right hip flexion causes pain in the right groin.
Motor bulk and tone are normal in lower extremities.
The doctor diagnoses right hip degenerative joint disease.
The doctor recommends low impact exercises, NSAIDs as needed, and use of a cane.
The doctor proposes a cortisone injection into the right hip.
The doctor will administer the injection today and schedule a follow-up in three months.
The doctor reviews the risks of the cortisone injection.
